name: LayerFolder
summary: Note: As of SketchUp 2020 “Layers” were renamed to “Tags” in the UI. The API retains the use of “Layer” for compatibility and is synonymous with “Tag”. Allows layers to be organized in folders. Folders may have duplicate names.

methods.name: <=>
methods.summary: The #<=> method is used to compare two layer folders based on their names.
methods.remarks: The #<=> method is used to compare two layer folders based on their names. This enables the Ruby Array#sort method to sort SketchUp layer folders.

returns.doc: -1 if the receiver should appear before other. 1 if the receiver should appear after other. 0 if the receiver and other are equal. nil if other is not comparable with the receiver.

methods.name: add_folder
methods.summary: The #add_folder method adds or moves a layer folder.
methods.remarks: The #add_folder method adds or moves a layer folder.

methods.name: add_layer
methods.summary: The #add_layer method adds a layer to a folder.
methods.remarks: The #add_layer method adds a layer to a folder.

methods.name: count_folders
methods.summary: The #count_folders method retrieves the number of child folders in the folder.
methods.remarks: The #count_folders method retrieves the number of child folders in the folder.

methods.name: count_layers
methods.signature: count_layers => Integer Also known as: length, size
methods.summary: The #count_layers method retrieves the number of layers in the folder.
methods.remarks: The #count_layers method retrieves the number of layers in the folder.

methods.name: each_folder
methods.summary: The #each_folder method is used to iterate through the folders that are direct children to the folder.
methods.remarks: The #each_folder method is used to iterate through the folders that are direct children to the folder.

methods.name: each_layer
methods.signature: each_layer {|layer| ... } => Object Also known as: each
methods.summary: The #each_layer method is used to iterate through the layers that are direct children to the folder.
methods.remarks: The #each_layer method is used to iterate through the layers that are direct children to the folder.

methods.name: folders
methods.summary: The #folders returns the direct child-folders of the folder.
methods.remarks: The #folders returns the direct child-folders of the folder.

methods.name: layers
methods.summary: The #layers method retrieves the child layers of a folder.
methods.remarks: The #layers method retrieves the child layers of a folder.

methods.name: remove_folder
methods.summary: The #remove_folder method removes the folder from the model.
methods.remarks: The #remove_folder method removes the folder from the model. All children are preserved, but move up one level.

throws.when: if the folder is not a direct child of the receiver.

methods.name: remove_layer
methods.summary: The #remove_layer method removes a layer from a folder.
methods.remarks: The #remove_layer method removes a layer from a folder. The layer will be parent to the layer manager.

methods.name: visible?
methods.summary: The #visible? method is used to determine if the layer folder is visible.
methods.remarks: The #visible? method is used to determine if the layer folder is visible.

methods.name: visible_on_new_pages?
methods.summary: The #visible_on_new_pages? method is used to determine if the layer folder is by default visible on new pages.
methods.remarks: The #visible_on_new_pages? method is used to determine if the layer folder is by default visible on new pages.

properties.summary: The #== method is used to determine if two layer folders are the same.
properties.remarks: The #== method is used to determine if two layer folders are the same.

properties.name: folder
properties.summary: The #folder method is used to return the parent layer folder of a layer folder.
properties.remarks: The #folder method is used to return the parent layer folder of a layer folder.

properties.name: name
properties.summary: The #name method gets the name of the folder.
properties.remarks: The #name method gets the name of the folder.

properties.name: visible
properties.summary: The #visible= method is used to set if the layer folder is visible.
properties.remarks: The #visible= method is used to set if the layer folder is visible.

properties.name: visible_on_new_pages
properties.summary: The #visible_on_new_pages= method is used to set if the layer folder is by default visible on new pages.
properties.remarks: The #visible_on_new_pages= method is used to set if the layer folder is by default visible on new pages.
